abort.already_configured: Entry with the serial {serial} is already configured
abort.reauth_failed: Could not find the configuration entry to reauthenticate
abort.reconfigure_failed: Could not find the configuration entry to reconfigure

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to CCU [{invalid_items}]. Please verify: 1) The hostname/IP address is correct, 2) The CCU is powered on and reachable, 3) No firewall is blocking the connection.
error.detection_failed: Cannot connect to CCU [{invalid_items}]. Please verify: 1) The hostname/IP address is correct, 2) The CCU is powered on and reachable, 3) Username and password are correct (case-sensitive), 4) The user has admin privileges on the CCU, 5) No firewall is blocking the connection.
error.interface_not_available: The following interfaces are not available on the CCU: [{invalid_items}]. These interfaces were not detected during backend discovery. Please disable them or verify they are installed and running on your CCU.
error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed for CCU [{invalid_items}]. Please verify: 1) Username and password are correct (case-sensitive), 2) The user has admin privileges on the CCU, 3) Password only contains allowed characters: A-Z, a-z, 0-9 and .!$():;#-
error.invalid_config: Invalid configuration: [{invalid_items}]. Please check the entered values and try again.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Please check the Home Assistant logs for more details.

progress.detect_backend: Detecting backend type and available interfaces...

data.use_group_channel_for_cover_state: Report cover position from group channel

data_description.callback_host: IP address for CCU callbacks. Leave empty for auto-detection.
data_description.callback_port_xml_rpc: Port for XML-RPC callbacks. Leave empty for automatic assignment.
data_description.command_throttle_interval: Minimum delay between device commands in seconds. Set to 0 to disable throttling. Ensures smooth operation for large installations.
data_description.enable_config_panel: Show a panel in the sidebar for editing Homematic device MASTER parameters.
data_description.enable_system_notifications: Show notifications for connection issues and device errors in Home Assistant.
data_description.light_last_brightness_enabled: When enabled, lights will turn on with the last non-zero brightness level instead of default 100%.
data_description.listen_on_all_ip: Enable if Home Assistant runs in Docker or a VM. Required when HA's IP differs from the host IP.
data_description.mqtt_enabled: Required for CUxD and CCU-Jack device support. Requires MQTT broker connected to Home Assistant.
data_description.mqtt_prefix: MQTT topic prefix configured in CCU-Jack. Leave empty if not using CCU-Jack MQTT bridge.
data_description.optional_settings: Enable experimental features to try out new functionality. These features may change or be removed in future versions.
data_description.program_markers: Filter programs by marker in description (HAHM, HX, INTERNAL). Leave empty to import all programs.
data_description.program_scan_enabled: Import CCU programs as button entities to trigger them from Home Assistant.
data_description.sub_devices_enabled: Splits devices with multiple channel groups into a main device with sub-devices. Entities are assigned to their corresponding sub-device based on channel group.
data_description.sysvar_markers: Filter system variables by marker in description (HAHM, HX, INTERNAL). Leave empty to import all variables.
data_description.sysvar_scan_enabled: Import CCU system variables as entities to read and write them from Home Assistant.
data_description.sysvar_scan_interval: Polling interval in seconds for system variables and programs (default: 30).
data_description.use_group_channel_for_cover_state: Use the group channel to report cover position. Enable this for covers that report incorrect positions on their individual channels.

data_description.instance_name: Unique identifier for this Home Assistant installation. Allows multiple Home Assistant instances to connect to the same CCU simultaneously. Has no relation to the CCU hostname/IP. Do not change after setup — entities would be recreated.
data_description.password: Case sensitive. Allowed characters: A-Z, a-z, 0-9 and .!$():;#-
data_description.skip_backend_detection: Enable this option if you use non-standard ports or if backend detection hangs. You will manually configure interfaces and ports in the next step.
data_description.username: Case sensitive. User must have administrator role

state.frequency_lowon_longoff_highon_longoff: Low on-long off, high on-long off

callback_server_failed.description:
The callback server for interface **{interface_id}** failed to start.

The integration cannot receive state updates from the CCU. Please check:
- The configured callback port is not in use by another application
- Network/firewall settings allow incoming connections
- Home Assistant has permission to bind to the port

Reload the integration to retry.
callback_server_failed.title: Callback server failed for {interface_id}

central_degraded.title: CCU connection degraded

central_failed.description:
The CCU connection for **{instance_name}** has failed.

Reason: {reason}

Automatic recovery attempts have been exhausted. Please check:
- CCU is powered on and reachable
- Network connectivity
- CCU logs for errors

Reload the integration to retry.
central_failed.title: CCU connection failed

central_failed_internal.description:
The CCU connection for **{instance_name}** has failed due to an internal CCU error.

Interface: {interface_id}

The CCU reported an internal error. Please:
- Check CCU system logs for errors
- Consider restarting the CCU
- Update CCU firmware if available

Reload the integration to retry.
central_failed_internal.title: CCU internal error

central_failed_network.description:
The CCU connection for **{instance_name}** has failed due to a network connectivity issue.

Interface: {interface_id}

Please check:
- CCU is powered on and reachable on the network
- Network cables and Wi-Fi connectivity
- Firewall settings allowing XML-RPC traffic
- CCU network configuration

Reload the integration to retry.
central_failed_network.title: CCU network connection failed

central_failed_timeout.description:
The CCU connection for **{instance_name}** has failed due to connection timeouts.

Interface: {interface_id}

The CCU may be overloaded or experiencing performance issues. Please check:
- CCU CPU and memory usage
- CCU is not executing long-running operations
- Network latency between Home Assistant and CCU

Reload the integration to retry.
central_failed_timeout.title: CCU connection timeout

client_failed.description:
The XML-RPC client for interface **{interface_id}** has disconnected.

The integration cannot send commands to the CCU for this interface. Please check:
- CCU is powered on and reachable
- Network connectivity to the CCU
- CCU logs for errors

The integration is attempting automatic reconnection.
client_failed.title: Client disconnected for {interface_id}

connection_failed.description:
The connection to interface **{interface_id}** has failed.

The integration cannot communicate with this interface. Please check:
- CCU is powered on and reachable
- The interface is enabled on the CCU
- Network/firewall settings

The integration is attempting automatic reconnection.
connection_failed.title: Connection failed for {interface_id}

data_description.device_name: Enter a name for the device. This will be used as the device name in the CCU and Home Assistant. Leave empty to keep the default name.

set_name.description:
A new device with address **{address}** was detected on interface **{interface_id}**.

Enter a name and confirm to add it to Home Assistant.
set_name.title: Add delayed device {address}

fetch_data_failed.description:
Failed to fetch device data from interface **{interface_id}**.

Unable to fetch data using a Rega script. This indicates an issue on your CCU.

The integration will use a fallback method, which may lead to higher DutyCycle during startup for this interface.
fetch_data_failed.title: Failed to fetch device data

incomplete_device_data.description:
Device data is incomplete for **{device_count}** device(s) on interface **{interface_id}**.

**Affected devices:** {device_addresses}

The CCU did not return all required paramsets for these devices. This may indicate:
- Devices are not fully initialized on the CCU
- Communication issues between the CCU and devices
- The devices need to be re-paired

The affected devices were not created. Try reloading the integration or restarting the CCU.
incomplete_device_data.title: Incomplete device data on {interface_id}

interface_not_reachable.description:
No connection to interface {interface_id}.

This Integration tries an automatic reconnect.

Check the Home Assistant log files for more details
interface_not_reachable.title: Interface {interface_id} not reachable

paramset_inconsistency.title: Device paramset inconsistency detected on {interface_id}

pending_pong_mismatch.description:
There is a mismatch between send ping events and received pong events for HA instance {instance_name}.

Possible reasons:
 - You are running multiple instances of HA with the same instance name configured for this integration. Re-add one instance! Otherwise this HA instance will not receive update events from your CCU.
 - Something is stuck on the CCU or hasn't been cleaned up. Therefore, try a restart.
 - Your setup is misconfigured and HA is not able to receive events from the CCU
pending_pong_mismatch.title: Pending Pong mismatch on interface {interface_id}

ping_pong_mismatch.description:
A ping-pong mismatch was detected on interface **{interface_id}**.

**Mismatch type:** {mismatch_type}
**Mismatch count:** {mismatch_count}

Possible reasons:
 - You are running multiple instances of Home Assistant with the same instance name configured for this integration. Re-add one instance! Otherwise this Home Assistant instance will not receive update events from your CCU.
 - Something is stuck on the CCU or hasn't been cleaned up. Try restarting the CCU.
 - Your setup is misconfigured and Home Assistant is not able to receive events from the CCU.
ping_pong_mismatch.title: Ping-pong mismatch on interface {interface_id}

unknown_pong_mismatch.description:
There are unknown pong events observed for HA instance {instance_name}.

Possible reasons:
 - You are running multiple instances of HA with the same instance name configured for this integration. Re-add one instance! Otherwise this HA instance will not receive update events from your CCU.
 - Something is stuck on the CCU or hasn't been cleaned up. Therefore, try a restart.
 - Your setup is misconfigured and HA is not able to receive events from the CCU
unknown_pong_mismatch.title: Unknown Pong mismatch on interface {interface_id}

xmlrpc_server_receives_no_events.description:
Callback for {interface_id} has not received events for {seconds_since_last_event}s.

This Integration tries an automatic reconnect
xmlrpc_server_receives_no_events.title: XmlRPC-Server received no events
